task: Build a webhook ingestion service for Stripe payment events
note: Recorded Spotter session. Timings are offsets in ms from replay start; the client paces playback. eventsSeen ticks show everything the agent did; cards are the few Spotter chose to surface.

timeline.text: Agent session started - task: build a webhook ingestion service

card.headline: Chose a queue over inline processing
card.what_happened: The webhook now replies instantly and queues the real work for later.
card.why_it_matters: Slow replies make Stripe retry - and retries mean the same payment gets processed twice.
card.question: Why does slow inline processing cause double payments?
card.answer: Stripe times out, assumes failure, and resends the event.

options.text: Stripe times out and resends the event
The handler runs twice by default

card.headline: Verify the signature before parsing
card.what_happened: Every webhook is HMAC-checked against the raw bytes before anything reads it.
card.why_it_matters: Without this, anyone who finds your URL can fake a 'payment succeeded'.
card.question: Why check the raw body instead of the parsed JSON?
card.answer: HMAC is computed over exact bytes - re-serialized JSON gives different bytes and a false mismatch.

card.headline: Retries back off - with jitter
card.what_happened: Failed jobs retry at 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, each nudged by a random delay.
card.why_it_matters: 500 jobs retrying in sync would hammer the service that's trying to recover.
card.question: What breaks with a plain 'retry every 5s' rule?
card.answer: Everything retries at the same instant, in waves - jitter spreads them out.

card.headline: Dedupe in the database, not the code
card.what_happened: Each event ID gets a UNIQUE constraint - duplicates simply fail to insert.
card.why_it_matters: A 'have I seen this?' check in code has a race window. The constraint doesn't.
card.question: Why isn't SELECT-then-INSERT enough?
card.answer: Two concurrent requests can both read 'not seen' before either writes.

options.text: SELECT is too slow for webhooks
Two requests can both read 'not seen' first

card.headline: Poison jobs get a dead-letter queue
card.what_happened: After max retries, failing jobs park in a DLQ instead of vanishing.
card.why_it_matters: The alternatives are an infinite retry loop or silent data loss.
card.question: What two failures does a DLQ prevent?
card.answer: Retry loops that clog the queue, and dropped jobs nobody ever sees.

timeline.text: Agent finished - 47 actions taken, 5 surfaced to you. That's the product: the filter, not the feed.
